reasoning:
The question asks whether Alassane Ouattara will declare his candidacy for the 2025 Ivorian presidential election before June 1, 2025. 
The knowledge cutoff is April 29, 2025.
Based on the latest reports (mid-March 2025), the ruling RHDP party has officially indicated that Ouattara will be designated as their candidate. The RHDP spokesperson stated on March 19, 2025, that Ouattara would be "soon invested". Additional reports note that an RHDP congress to formally nominate him is expected in May 2025. 
The deadline for the question is June 1, 2025. Since the party's congress is typically the venue for such announcements and is scheduled for May 2025, there is a very high probability that Ouattara will officially declare his candidacy during that month. He has been giving strong hints since late 2024 and early 2025 that he is ready to run for a fourth term, citing his good health and stating he will serve if his party wants him to. The party clearly wants him to, having called him the "natural candidate". 
The likelihood of him stepping down at the last minute seems low, as no other RHDP successor has been groomed since the death of Gon Coulibaly in 2020. However, there is a small chance the congress could be delayed until June or later, or that he waits to formally declare until closer to the election (October 2025). Taking these factors into account, I estimate a 88% probability that he will declare before June 1, 2025.
